name: Razorpay Merchant Dashboard — Spring GraphQL API
scenario: Razorpay's frontend team makes 5 REST API calls per merchant dashboard page load. Replace with a Spring for GraphQL API that lets the web and mobile apps each request exactly the fields they need.

core.description: schema.graphqls in src/main/resources/graphql/: defines Payment, Merchant, Refund types. PaymentStatus enum. CreatePaymentInput and InitiateRefundInput input types. Query and Mutation types.
@Controller PaymentGraphQlController with @QueryMapping payment(id), @QueryMapping payments(merchantId, status). @Argument on all parameters.
@MutationMapping createPayment(@Argument CreatePaymentInput input), @MutationMapping initiateRefund(@Argument InitiateRefundInput input). Input type deserialization via @Argument.
@SchemaMapping(typeName='Payment') merchant(Payment payment) — field resolver. Called per payment in result set (document N+1 risk in comments).
ProductNotFoundException or PaymentNotFoundException extends RuntimeException. @Component DataFetcherExceptionResolverAdapter that maps it to ErrorType.NOT_FOUND.
@SpringBootTest or @GraphQlTest (4 tests): payment query, payments list, createPayment mutation, merchant field resolver. All 4 pass.
application.yml: spring.graphql.graphiql.enabled=true. Comment in README: how to access GraphiQL at localhost:8080/graphiql.

bonus.description: Pagination: payments(merchantId, status, page=0, size=20) returns PaymentPage { items, total, page, hasNext }. Service implements skip/limit logic. Test: page 0 returns items, hasNext=true when more exist.
Authentication context: @QueryMapping myPayments() — gets merchantId from GraphQlContext (not from @Argument). Test: with context returns correct payments, without context returns empty.
@GraphQlTest with GraphQlTester: .document(query).execute().path('payment.status').entity(String.class).isEqualTo('CREATED'). Mock the service layer with @MockBean.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Schema location: src/main/resources/graphql/schema.graphqls (Spring reads all *.graphqls files from this directory at startup). @QueryMapping method name = schema Query field name by default — if they differ, use @QueryMapping(name='fieldName'). @Argument for input types: Spring auto-deserializes the GraphQL input map to the Java record using field name matching. @SchemaMapping typeName: must exactly match the GraphQL type name in the schema (case-sensitive). Error handling: return null from @QueryMapping for 'not found' if schema field is nullable (Product); throw exception if non-null (Product!) — map exception to GraphQL error via DataFetcherExceptionResolverAdapter.
